2.2. Synthesis of gold nanoparticles Gold nanoparticles of 35, 70 and 200 nm average sizes were synthesized by the previously published method pioneered by Turkevich et al., depending on reducing hydrogen tetrachloroau- rate with sodium tricitrate (Turkevich et al., 1951).

Synthesis of gold nanoparticles Gold nanoparticles of 35, 70 and 200 nm average sizes were synthesized by the previously published method pioneered by Turkevich et al., depending on reducing hydrogen tetrachloroau- rate with sodium tricitrate (Turkevich et al., 1951). All the glass tools used in the preparation were washed first by aqua regia (3:1 v/v solution of conc HCl and conc HNO ) 3 and then rinsed with deionized water for three times and dried before usage.

2.3. Preparation of gold nanoparticles-carbapenem antibiotic conjugates Carbapenem conjugated gold nanoparticles were prepared by introducing 5 mL of the antibiotics (1 mg/mL in PBS, pH = 7.4) drop wise to the stirred 10 mL of the synthesized gold nanoparticles (0.1 mg/ml in PBS, pH = 7.4).

As well, the antibacterial action of meropenem conjugat- ed gold nanoparticles is greater than that of imipenem conjugated gold nanoparticles (Tables 2 and 3), which is mainly attributed to the remarkable superiority of meropenem over imipenem in inhibiting the bacterial cell wall synthesis (Cornaglia et al., 1992; Nicolau, 2008).

As well, the antibacterial action of meropenem conjugat- ed gold nanoparticles is greater than that of imipenem conjugated gold nanoparticles (Tables 2 and 3), which is mainly attributed to the remarkable superiority of meropenem over imipenem in inhibiting the bacterial cell wall synthesis (Cornaglia et al., 1992; Nicolau, 2008).
